title: Full mouse interactivity across the Go TUI — LazyGit-style clickable UI
intent: The Go TUI dashboard should feel as mouse-native as LazyGit. Every visible interactive element — tabs, task list items, subtask checkboxes, status badges, navigation hints, scroll regions, action buttons — must respond to mouse clicks. The user should never need to remember a keyboard shortcut to navigate; clicking should just work everywhere.
bridge_problem: Bubbletea supports mouse events natively (tea.EnableMouseAllMotion or tea.EnableMouseCellMotion), but the TUI likely doesn't process them or map click coordinates to UI regions. Every component needs hit-testing: given a click at (x,y), which element was clicked?
representation_layer: Component layout coordinates (position, width, height of each rendered element)
Zone-based hit testing (bubbletea-zone or manual coordinate mapping)

hypotheses.text: Bubbletea's built-in mouse support (tea.WithMouseCellMotion) can be enabled with minimal changes to the program init
The charmbracelet/bubblezone library provides zone-based click detection that maps click coordinates to named regions without manual coordinate math
Mouse scroll events can replace keyboard up/down for all scrollable lists and detail views
Click feedback (highlight on click, cursor change) can be done with Lipgloss style changes on the clicked element

constraints: Keyboard navigation must continue to work — mouse is additive, not a replacement
Must not break existing Bubbletea update/view cycle
Mouse support must be enabled by default (not opt-in)
Must work in tmux (tmux supports mouse with 'set -g mouse on')
No external dependencies beyond charmbracelet ecosystem (bubblezone is OK)
Click targets must be generous — not pixel-precise, forgiving hit zones
success_criteria: Tab bar: clicking a tab switches to that view
Task list: clicking a task selects it and opens detail view
Subtasks: clicking a subtask selects/toggles it
Scroll: mouse wheel scrolls any scrollable region (task list, detail view, activity log)
Back/close: clicking outside a detail view or on a close area dismisses it
Status badges: clicking a status badge opens status change if applicable
Action hints at bottom: clicking an action hint triggers that action
All interactions feel responsive — no lag, immediate visual feedback
evidence_plan: Research how LazyGit implements mouse support in Bubbletea (it uses gocui, but the pattern applies)
Test charmbracelet/bubblezone for zone-based click detection
Verify tmux mouse passthrough works with tea.WithMouseCellMotion
Test mouse wheel scrolling in nested Bubbletea components
Verify no regressions in keyboard navigation after adding mouse
deliverables: Mouse support enabled in the Bubbletea program
All list views respond to click-to-select
All scrollable regions respond to mouse wheel
Detail views respond to click interactions (subtasks, collapse, close)
Bottom action bar responds to clicks
Visual feedback on click (subtle highlight or state change)
Go build passes, no keyboard regressions

dispatch_plan.research_brief: Research how bubblezone and tea.WithMouseCellMotion work together, find examples of Bubbletea apps with full mouse support, document the pattern for hit-testing click coordinates against rendered component regions

phase_plan.scope: Investigate bubblezone, Bubbletea mouse API, LazyGit patterns
Enable mouse in tea.Program, set up zone manager, add mouse event routing to Update()
Add click handlers to each component: tab bar, task list, detail view, subtasks, activity log, action bar
Scroll support, click feedback, generous hit zones, tmux testing
